get.summary:
GetNetworkGraph returns a snapshot of observed network flows for all
deployments in the given cluster.
get.description:
Flows are filtered to those observed after the `since` timestamp (default:
5 minutes ago). The result graph includes deployment nodes, external source
nodes, and directed edges with per-port connection metadata when
`include_ports` is true.

Deployments invisible to the caller due to Scoped Access Control are masked
(replaced with anonymized nodes) rather than omitted, to preserve graph
topology. Flows between two invisible deployments are omitted entirely.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty or the number of matching
deployments exceeds the configured maximum.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist or is not visible.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the ID of the cluster to retrieve the network graph for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters deployments shown in the graph using StackRox search syntax.
Example: "Namespace:production+Label:app=frontend"

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
since restricts flows to those observed after this timestamp.
Defaults to 5 minutes before the current time if unset.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description:
include_ports controls whether per-port connection details are included in
edge properties.

parameters.name: scope.query
parameters.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

parameters.name: includePolicies
parameters.description:
include_policies controls whether NetworkPolicy isolation information
(non_isolated_ingress, non_isolated_egress, policy_ids) is populated on
each deployment node.

get.summary:
GetExternalNetworkEntities returns the user-defined external network
entities (CIDR blocks) visible for the given cluster, optionally filtered
by the search query.
get.description:
Results include both cluster-scoped entities and global (cluster-agnostic)
entities. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query is malformed.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id restricts results to entities scoped to this cluster (or global entities).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query further filters entities using StackRox search syntax.

post.summary:
CreateExternalNetworkEntity creates a new user-defined external network
entity (CIDR block) scoped to the given cluster.
post.description:
The entity ID is derived deterministically from the cluster ID and CIDR
block. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cluster ID or CIDR is invalid.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist or is not visible.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to which this external entity is scoped. Required.

get.summary:
GetExternalNetworkFlowsMetadata returns per-entity flow counts for all
external network entities that have observed flows in the cluster.
get.description:
Results are sorted by entity ID and support pagination. Useful for
building summary views without fetching full flow details.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to query. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters deployments using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
since restricts flows to those observed after this timestamp.
Defaults to 5 minutes before the current time if unset.

get.summary:
GetExternalNetworkFlows returns the observed network flows between cluster
deployments and a specific external network entity.
get.description:
Flows are paginated and sorted by source entity ID for deterministic
pagination. Only flows involving at least one deployment visible to the
caller are returned.

Returns NOT_FOUND if the external entity does not exist.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to query for external flows. Required.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: entity_id is the ID of the external network entity to retrieve flows for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query additionally filters the set of deployments considered, using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
since restricts flows to those observed after this timestamp.
Defaults to 5 minutes before the current time if unset.

get.summary: GetNetworkGraphConfig returns the current network graph display configuration.
get.description:
The configuration controls settings such as whether default external
network sources are hidden in the graph. Requires View access to
the Administration resource.

put.summary: PutNetworkGraphConfig replaces the network graph display configuration.
put.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the config body is missing.
Requires Modify access to the Administration resource.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: PutNetworkGraphConfigRequest wraps the new network graph configuration to persist.

delete.summary: DeleteExternalNetworkEntity removes a user-defined external network entity.
delete.description:
Only user-created entities can be deleted; StackRox-generated default
entities are immutable and will return PERMISSION_DENIED.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the entity does not exist.

patch.summary:
PatchExternalNetworkEntity renames an existing user-defined external
network entity.
patch.description:
Only user-created entities can be updated; StackRox-generated default
entities are immutable and will return PERMISSION_DENIED.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the entity ID is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the entity does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the ID of the external network entity to update. Required.

default.description: `default` indicates whether the external source is user-generated or system-generated.

discovered.description: `discovered` indicates whether the external source is harvested from monitored traffic.

NetworkEntityInfoExternalSource.description: Update normalizeDupNameExtSrcs(...) in `central/networkgraph/aggregator/aggregator.go` whenever this message is updated.

entity.description: entity contains the CIDR block and optional name for the new external source.

NetworkGraphServiceCreateExternalNetwork.description:
CreateNetworkEntityRequest describes a new user-defined external network
entity (CIDR block) scoped to a specific cluster.

name.description: name is the new display name for the entity.

NetworkGraphServicePatchExternalNetworkE.description: PatchNetworkEntityRequest renames an existing user-defined external network entity.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

scope.description:
`scope` represents known cluster network peers to which the flows must be scoped. In future, to restrict flows
to more granular entities, such as deployment, scope could include deployment ID.
Note: The highest scope level is cluster.

storageNetworkEntityInfoType.title: - INTERNAL_ENTITIES: INTERNAL_ENTITIES is for grouping all internal entities under a single network graph node

clusterId.title: Need the clusterID as that is part of the key in RocksDB

updatedAt.title:
This field is set by Central everytime a flow is upserted.
This should not be set by Sensor.
For more context: https://github.com/stackrox/stackrox/pull/14483

srcEntity.title: The source deployment ID of the network flow

dstEntity.title: The destination deployment ID of the network flow

dstPort.description: may be 0 if not applicable (e.g., icmp).

entity.description: entity is the external network entity.

flowsCount.description: flowsCount is the total number of observed flows involving this entity.

v1ExternalNetworkFlowMetadata.description:
ExternalNetworkFlowMetadata summarizes observed flow counts for a single
external network entity.

entities.description:
entities is the paginated list of external entities with their flow counts,
sorted by entity ID for deterministic pagination.

totalEntities.description: total_entities is the total number of external entities before pagination.

v1GetExternalNetworkFlowsMetadataRespons.description:
GetExternalNetworkFlowsMetadataResponse contains per-entity flow counts for
all external entities with observed flows.

entity.description: entity is the external network entity these flows are associated with.

totalFlows.description: total_flows is the total number of matching flows before pagination.

flows.description: flows is the paginated list of observed network flows.

v1GetExternalNetworkFlowsResponse.description:
GetExternalNetworkFlowsResponse contains flows between cluster deployments
and a single external network entity.

port.description: port is the destination port of the connection.

protocol.description: protocol is the Layer-4 protocol (TCP, UDP, etc.) of the connection.

lastActiveTimestamp.description: last_active_timestamp is the most recent time this connection was observed.

v1NetworkEdgeProperties.description:
NetworkEdgeProperties describes a single observed connection on a specific
port and protocol.

v1NetworkEdgePropertiesBundle.description:
NetworkEdgePropertiesBundle groups all connection properties observed on a
single directed edge between two network nodes.

epoch.description:
epoch is a monotonically increasing counter that increments whenever the
underlying network policy graph changes. Clients can compare epochs to
detect staleness without re-fetching the full graph.

nodes.description:
nodes is the list of network entities in the graph. Edge indices in
out_edges refer to positions in this list.

v1NetworkGraph.description:
NetworkGraph is the response to GetNetworkGraph, containing a snapshot of
observed network flows within a cluster.

query.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

v1NetworkGraphScope.description:
NetworkGraphScope restricts the network graph to a subset of deployments
matching the given search query.

entity.description: entity identifies the network entity (deployment, CIDR block, internet, etc.).

internetAccess.description: internet_access is true if this node has observed flows to/from the internet.

policyIds.description:
policy_ids lists the IDs of NetworkPolicies that select this deployment.
Populated only when include_policies is set in the request.

nonIsolatedIngress.description:
non_isolated_ingress is true when no ingress NetworkPolicy selects this deployment,
meaning all ingress traffic is allowed.

nonIsolatedEgress.description:
non_isolated_egress is true when no egress NetworkPolicy selects this deployment,
meaning all egress traffic is allowed.

queryMatch.description:
query_match is true if this node matched the filter query in the request
(as opposed to appearing only as a neighbor of a matching node).

outEdges.description:
out_edges maps the index of a destination node in the nodes list to the
set of observed connection properties on that edge.

v1NetworkNode.description:
NetworkNode represents a single entity (deployment, external source, or
internet) in the network graph.

sortOptions.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.

config.description: config is the new network graph configuration. Required.

v1PutNetworkGraphConfigRequest.description: PutNetworkGraphConfigRequest wraps the new network graph configuration to persist.

aggregateBy.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.
